patches.find: The cleanest independence test is the raw Gaia astrometric residuals — the annual sinusoidal position signature has amplitude that correlates with spectroscopic-class distance estimates (which set absolute magnitudes from spectral classification rather than from a parallax-calibrated period-luminosity relation), confirming that the 1/d pattern is in the geometry of the data and not an artifact of the photometric calibration chain.
patches.replace: A second independence check: cross-validation against spectroscopic-class distance estimates (which set absolute magnitudes from stellar spectral classification rather than from a parallax-calibrated period-luminosity relation) shows the same 1/d scaling, confirming that the parallax pattern is in the geometry of the data and not an artifact of the photometric calibration chain.
patches.rationale: WIN-017.c5 holes H1 (major: uncited spectroscopic-class distance claim) + H2 (moderate: competing 'cleanest'/'even stronger' superlatives) + H4 (minor: misleading 'raw Gaia astrometric residuals' terminology). Three-in-one fix: removes the uncited empirical assertion about a specific amplitude-vs-distance correlation, eliminates the 'cleanest' superlative that competed with 'even stronger evidence' in the next sentence, and replaces the technically inaccurate 'raw Gaia astrometric residuals' framing. The scientific point (photometric-calibration independence) is preserved by rephrasing as cross-validation showing 1/d scaling. Citation guidance for future strengthening: Lindegren et al. 2021 A&A 649 A2 (EDR3 astrometric solution) discusses independence checks vs photometric distances.

patches.find: with no derivation of azimuthal asymmetry and no published functional form
patches.replace: with no derivation of azimuthal asymmetry (MHD attributes the African–American asymmetry to LLSVP-modulated core flow — a structural mechanism the dome’s radially symmetric aetheric vortex cannot reproduce, regardless of when the prediction was made) and no published functional form
patches.rationale: WIN-004.c5 hole H1 (moderate): c4's LLSVP parenthetical recommendation was partially addressed but LLSVP attribution was attenuated in the patch translation. Inserting the LLSVP/timing-independence parenthetical directly after 'no derivation of azimuthal asymmetry' completes the closure: names LLSVP explicitly, asserts the radial-symmetry falsification, and makes the timing-independence explicit. Reduces advocate defense_survives from 3 to 1-2 per c5 review.

patches.find: MHD core dynamics explain SAA bifurcation mechanism (reversed-flux patches at CMB); dome has no fluid mechanism; bifurcation rate in detail evidence is observational, not first-principles-derived
patches.replace: MHD core dynamics explain SAA bifurcation mechanism (reversed-flux patches at CMB); dome has no fluid mechanism; bifurcation rate in detail evidence is observational, not first-principles-derived; African–American decay asymmetry falsifies a radially symmetric aetheric vortex regardless of prediction timing
patches.rationale: WIN-004.c5 stronger_argument #1 (carried from c4): append asymmetry kill-shot to summary finding so skim readers see structural-falsification framing, not just mechanism-comparison. Converts finding from 195 to ~285 chars. Closes the hole that readers who never expand the panel only see the rate-acknowledgment concession and miss the asymmetry falsification.
